member.name: Jodie Gosling

addresses: Town Hall, Coton Road, Nuneaton, CV11 5AA

member.synopsis: Jodie Gosling is the Labour MP for Nuneaton , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Incontinence: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what plans he has to improve referral rates for female urinary incontinence for women from minority-ethnic backgrounds.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The data held on referrals to gynaecology services is not broken down by the reason of referral. This means it is not possible to identify referrals specifically for female urinary incontinence, either for the total population or for women from minority e...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Incontinence: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of trends in the level of referral rates for female urinary incontinence for women from minority-ethnic backgrounds.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The data held on referrals to gynaecology services is not broken down by the reason of referral. This means it is not possible to identify referrals specifically for female urinary incontinence, either for the total population or for women from minority e...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Incontinence: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve data collection and reporting of rates of treatment in women with urinary continence from minority-ethnic backgrounds.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The data held on referrals to gynaecology services is not broken down by the reason of referral. This means it is not possible to identify referrals specifically for female urinary incontinence, either for the total population or for women from minority e...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Incontinence: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the referral rate to hospital for female urinary incontinence treatment is for (a) the total population and (b) women from minority-ethnic backgrounds.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The data held on referrals to gynaecology services is not broken down by the reason of referral. This means it is not possible to identify referrals specifically for female urinary incontinence, either for the total population or for women from minority e...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Allied Health Professions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what proportion of senior NHS leadership roles are held by Allied Health Professionals; and what steps he is taking to increase that proportion.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department and NHS England do not currently hold a comprehensive central dataset on the proportion of all senior NHS Board-level roles held by Allied Health Professionals (AHPs).NHS England is actively seeking to increase representation from a broader...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Water: Cemeteries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of burial sites on downstream water quality during heavy rainfall.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra recognises that poorly located or managed burial sites can, in some circumstances, pose a risk to water quality, particularly during periods of heavy rainfall. At the same time, we understand that burial grounds are deeply important places for famil...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Allied Health Professions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department plans to take to retain the chief allied health professions officer role following the abolition of NHS England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The proposed abolition of NHS England and associated transfer and modification of functions is subject to legislation and the will of Parliament. We will be engaging with partners inside and outside the organisations as the process to design the future De...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy Company Obligation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what recent steps his Department has taken to rectify poor quality works undertaken as part of the Energy Company Obligation 4 scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are offering an audit to all households that received external wall insulation (EWI) measures under ECO4 and GBIS schemes. Since this became fully operational in January, over 9,000 properties have been contacted, with over 3,000 audits completed. The ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Medical Certificates: Health Professions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether (a) physiotherapists, (b) occupational therapists, (c) nurses, (d) pharmacists, and (e) other healthcare professionals who have been eligible to issue fit notes since July 2022 will be issuing f
writtenQuestions.answerText: All healthcare professionals currently eligible to issue fit notes will continue to be able to do so. The Government is not bringing forward any changes to fit note legislation as part of the fit note reform pilots. The four fit note pilots will test a vo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Physiotherapy: Community Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the NHS's document entitled Neighbourhood health centre guidance for regions and integrated care boards published on 16 April 2026, whether physiotherapists are included in the co
writtenQuestions.answerText: Neighbourhood health centres (NHCs) will bring together general practice (GP) with a mix of community, local authority, adult social care, and civil society services, allowing staff to deliver more coordinated and effective care for better patient outcome...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Allergies: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has considered exempting information on how to use adrenaline devices from restrictions only allowing medical professionals to access information about prescription medicines
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following the approval of an adrenaline nasal spray by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) in July 2025, the Government is considering whether any amendments to Schedule 17 of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 are appropriate...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Allergies: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has considered the potential merits of legislating to allow for community access to adrenaline.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following the approval of an adrenaline nasal spray by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) in July 2025, the Government is considering whether any amendments to Schedule 17 of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 are appropriate...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Allergies: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if his Department will update Schedule 17 of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 to allow for use of adrenaline nasal sprays.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following the approval of an adrenaline nasal spray by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) in July 2025, the Government is considering whether any amendments to Schedule 17 of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 are appropriate...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Iran: Baha'i Faith

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 19 May in response to Question 611.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Reserve Forces
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of current entry requirements for the armed forces reserve; and whether his Department plans to review entry requirements for the armed forces reserves.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence (MOD) continually assesses the entry requirements for Reserve recruitment and is actively streamlining joining processes, along with many other aspects of the Reserves, to ensure they support national resilience. This Government ha...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Juries: Chronic Illnesses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether he has made an assessment of the potential merits of introducing a permanent exemption to Jury service for people suffering from lifelong or degenerative conditions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has not made a specific assessment of introducing a permanent exemption for people with lifelong or degenerative conditions. Jury service is an important civic duty, and the Government is committed to ensuring that disability is never a bar...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Drugs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the planned timeline is for publishing the next iteration of the taxonomy being developed as part of the ongoing reforms to Drug Tariff Part IX.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The final draft, version 1.2, of the Part IX Drug Tariff (Med Tech in the community) Wave 1 categorisation was developed in collaboration with an Expert Reference Group and updated in line with stakeholder feedback over four iterations. The final version ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
